rag_rules: Place generated AGREE annex blocks in the target component or implementation scope, following AADL architecture rules
Preserve existing AADL architecture when inserting AGREE annex blocks
Use visible identifiers when available, and avoid implementation-only subcomponent paths in component type contracts
Place input envelope assumptions and output range guarantees in component type annexes when concrete bounds or visible relations are available
generic_rules: Insert AGREE annex blocks in the appropriate scope to maintain architectural integrity
Preserve existing AADL architecture when fusing AGREE contracts
Use visible identifiers when available, and avoid implementation-only subcomponent paths in component type contracts
Place input envelope assumptions and output range guarantees in component type annexes when concrete bounds or visible relations are available
syntax_or_pattern_reminders: Use AGREE logical operators such as `and`, `or`, `not(...)`, and implication `=>`
Use initialized temporal recurrence for stateful equations, for example `0.0 -> pre(t) + step`
Use nested `if ... then ... else` for branching logic
Use AGREE scalar declaration types: int, real, and bool
anti_patterns: Do not use implementation-only subcomponent paths in component type contracts
Do not place input envelope assumptions and output range guarantees in component type annexes without concrete bounds or visible relations
Do not use range comparisons or 0/1 equality over Boolean, enumeration-like, or opaque data ports unless the requirement and AADL data representation provide a numeric scalar
irrelevant_topics: AGREE stateful equations and branching logic patterns
AGREE scalar type names and comparison expressions
Assumed type vs actual type alignment
